explanations.6: The backslash at the end of this line tells Docker to continue the command on the next line, so all three apt-get commands are chained together with && (meaning if one fails, the rest won't run).
explanations.10: This line cleans up the temporary package lists that apt-get created, which reduces the final Docker image size by removing files we don't need anymore.
explanations.23: The sed command (stream editor) is being used to uncomment certain lines in Apache's configuration file by finding lines that start with # and removing that # character.
explanations.25: This uses a regular expression pattern to find lines starting with '#Include' and removes the '#' so that line becomes active in the Apache config.
explanations.27: Similar to line 25, this uncomments the line that loads the SSL module so Apache can handle HTTPS traffic.
explanations.35: The a2enmod command enables Apache modules (in this case SSL for HTTPS support), and the && chains multiple commands so they all run in sequence.
